MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Scott I. Bissin, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Prakash Wati Kumar, who was placed under oath but did not testify.
Daypitambar Chand, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath but did not testify.
Default was entered against defendant Dutt Shakuntala on September 26, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant Yugendra Singh, believed to be deceased, and the Testate and Intestate successors of Yugendra Singh on September 26, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant Dutt Shakuntala on September 26, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant Bisun Dutt (Deceased) and the Testate and Intestate successors of Bisun Dutt on September 26, 2017.
Dismissal as to defendant the Testate and Intestate successor of Bob Dharam Pitambar (Deceased) was filed on November 3, 2016.
Dismissal as to All Other Persons Unknown, Claiming Any Legal or Equitable Right, Title, Estate, Lien, or Interest in the Property described in the Complaint was entered on September 26, 2017.
Doe defendants were dismissed on September 22, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Prakash Wati Kumar, Trustee of the Kumar Power of Appointment Trust Under Trust Agreement dated September 12, 2003 and Daya Pitambar, a/k/a Dayawati Pitabar, a/k/a Dayawati Chand, and against Bob Dharam Pitambar and Deborah Gail Pitambar, Husband and Wife as joint tenants, the Testate and Intestate successors of Bob Dharam Pitambar (Deceased), et al., as follows:

See Interlocutory Judgment for Partition and Quiet Judgment, which the Court shall sign once the cross-complaint has been dismissed.

Matter is CONTINUED to January 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. for confirmation that the cross-complaint has been dismissed and for execution of the Interlocutory Judgment for Partition and Quiet Title Judgment.
